title: Cystopteris obtusa
intro: Cystopteris obtusa, also known as the ladder fern or thread fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Cystopteris obtusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cystopteris obtusa is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Cystopteris obtusa is classified within the family Cystopteridaceae. This family is part of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The order Polypodiales is a group of ferns that are vascular plants. Cystopteris obtusa is a member of the genus Cystopteris, which contains several species of ferns. The classification of Cystopteris obtusa is further refined at the species level.
uses: Cystopteris obtusa, also known as the fragile fern, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although these uses require further scientific research for confirmation. Some studies suggest that Cystopteris obtusa may have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of this plant.

parts: Cystopteris obtusa, also known as the fragile fern or oblong-leaved brittle fern, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. side_effects: Cystopteris obtusa is not typically used for medicinal purposes, but it's essential to note that the plant can cause irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly if ingested or touched.
